title: Flankers-FAR
description: This dataset comprises event-related potential (ERP) recordings from a visual word recognition task employing a flanker manipulation paradigm. Participants viewed four-letter words and pseudowords presented with varying flanker conditions (no flanker, identical flankers, or different flankers) while seated in a sound-attenuated room. The study investigates how flanking stimuli modulate neural responses to lexical processing, with 270 trials per participant collected at the NeuroCognition Laboratory at San Diego State University.
methods_description: Data were collected using EEG recording while participants performed a visual word recognition task. Participants sat in a darkened, sound-attenuated room at a viewing distance of 152 cm from a 24-inch LCD monitor. Stimuli consisted of four-letter words and pseudowords presented in white font on a black background, with flanker words separated by 3.28 degrees of visual angle. Each trial began with a purple fixation cross (1000 ms), followed by a white fixation cross (500 ms), stimulus presentation (150 ms), and a blank screen until participant response via game controller.
